heading: Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Program

the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: A
children.text: is complete or substantially complete;

children.label: i
children.text: at least 50,000 acres;

children.label: ii
children.text: comprised primarily of forested National Forest System land, but may also include land under the jurisdiction of the Bureau of Land Management, land under the jurisdiction of the Bureau of Indian Affairs, or other Federal, State, tribal, or private land;

children.label: iii
children.text: in need of active ecosystem restoration; and

children.label: iv
children.text: accessible by existing or proposed wood-processing infrastructure at an appropriate scale to use woody biomass and small-diameter wood removed in ecological restoration treatments;

children.label: C
children.text: incorporates the best available science and scientific application tools in ecological restoration strategies;

children.label: D
children.text: fully maintains, or contributes toward the restoration of, the structure and composition of old growth stands according to the pre-fire suppression old growth conditions characteristic of the forest type, taking into account the contribution of the stand to landscape fire adaptation and watershed health and retaining the large trees contributing to old growth structure;

children.label: i
children.text: focusing on small diameter trees, thinning, strategic fuel breaks, and fire use to modify fire behavior, as measured by the projected reduction of uncharacteristically severe wildfire effects for the forest type (such as adverse soil impacts, tree mortality or other impacts); and

children.label: ii
children.text: maximizing the retention of large trees, as appropriate for the forest type, to the extent that the trees promote fire-resilient stands; and

children.label: i
children.text: does not include the establishment of permanent roads; and

children.label: ii
children.text: would commit funding to decommission all temporary roads constructed to carry out the strategy;

children.label: A
children.text: includes multiple interested persons representing diverse interests; and

children.label: i
children.text: is transparent and nonexclusive; or

children.label: ii
children.text: meets the requirements for a resource advisory committee under subsections (c) through (f) of ;section 7125 of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: reduce the risk of uncharacteristic wildfire, including through the use of fire for ecological restoration and maintenance and reestablishing natural fire regimes, where appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: improve fish and wildlife habitat, including for endangered, threatened, and sensitive species;

children.label: C
children.text: maintain or improve water quality and watershed function;

children.label: D
children.text: prevent, remediate, or control invasions of exotic species;

children.label: E
children.text: maintain, decommission, and rehabilitate roads and trails;

children.label: F
children.text: use woody biomass and small-diameter trees produced from projects implementing the strategy;

children.label: G
children.text: report annually on performance, including through performance measures from the plan entitled the “10 Year Comprehensive Strategy Implementation Plan” and dated December 2006; and

children.label: H
children.text: take into account any applicable community wildfire protection plan;

children.label: A
children.text: reduced wildfire management costs; and

children.label: B
children.text: a decrease in the unit costs of implementing ecological restoration treatments over time;

children.label: A
children.text: the annual Federal funding necessary to implement the proposal; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of new non-Federal investment for carrying out the proposal that would be leveraged;

children.label: A
children.text: participation by or consultation with State, local, and Tribal governments; and

children.label: B
children.text: any established record of successful collaborative planning and implementation of ecological restoration projects on National Forest System land and other land included in the proposal by the collaborators; and

children.label: C
children.text: existing or proposed small or micro-businesses, clusters, or incubators; or

children.label: D
children.text: other entities that will hire or train local people to complete such contracts, grants, or agreements; and
be subject to any other requirements that the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior, determines to be necessary for the efficient and effective administration of the program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A Regional Forester may nominate for selection by the Secretary any proposals that meet the eligibility criteria established by subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: State Director of the Bureau of Land Management;

children.label: ii
children.text: Regional Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs; or

children.label: iii
children.text: other official of the Department of the Interior.

children.label: i
children.text: include a plan to use Federal funds allocated to the region to fund those costs of planning and carrying out ecological restoration treatments on National Forest System land, consistent with the strategy, that would not be covered by amounts transferred to the Secretary from the Fund; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide evidence that amounts proposed to be transferred to the Secretary from the Fund during the first 2 fiscal years following selection would be used to carry out ecological restoration treatments consistent with the strategy during the same fiscal year in which the funds are transferred to the Secretary;

children.label: i
children.text: State Director of the Bureau of Land Management;

children.label: ii
children.text: Regional Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs; or

children.label: iii
children.text: other official of the Department of the Interior; and

children.label: C
children.text: if actions on land not under the jurisdiction of the Secretary or the Secretary of the Interior are proposed, the appropriate Regional Forester shall provide evidence that the landowner intends to participate in, and provide appropriate funding to carry out, the actions.

children.label: A
children.text: have been nominated under subsection (c)(2); and

children.label: B
children.text: meet the eligibility criteria established by subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: the strength of the proposal and strategy;

children.label: B
children.text: the strength of the ecological case of the proposal and the proposed ecological restoration strategies;

children.label: C
children.text: the strength of the collaborative process and the likelihood of successful collaboration throughout implementation;

children.label: D
children.text: whether the proposal is likely to achieve reductions in long-term wildfire management costs;

children.label: E
children.text: whether the proposal would reduce the relative costs of carrying out ecological restoration treatments as a result of the use of woody biomass and small-diameter trees; and

children.label: F
children.text: whether an appropriate level of non-Federal investment would be leveraged in carrying out the proposal.

children.label: A
children.text: 10 proposals to be funded during any fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: 2 proposals in any 1 region of the National Forest System to be funded during any fiscal year; and

children.label: C
children.text: the number of proposals that the Secretary determines are likely to receive adequate funding.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), after consulting with the advisory panel established under subsection (e), if the Secretary determines that a proposal that has been selected under paragraph (1) and is being carried out continues to meet the eligibility criteria established by subsection (b), the Secretary, on a case-by-case basis, may issue for the proposal a 1-time extension of the 10-year period requirement under paragraph (1)(B) of that subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be for the shortest period of time practicable to complete implementation of the proposal, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not exceed 10 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and maintain an advisory panel comprised of not more than 15 members to evaluate, and provide recommendations on, each proposal that has been nominated under subsection (c)(2).

children.heading: Representation
children.label: 2 Representation
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the membership of the advisory panel is fairly balanced in terms of the points of view represented and the functions to be performed by the advisory panel.

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: 3 Inclusion
children.text: The advisory panel shall include experts in ecological restoration, fire ecology, fire management, rural economic development, strategies for ecological adaptation to climate change, fish and wildlife ecology, and woody biomass and small-diameter tree utilization.

children.heading: Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Fund
children.label: f Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Fund

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund, to be known as the “Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Fund”, to be used to pay up to 50 percent of the cost of carrying out and monitoring ecological restoration treatments on National Forest System land for each proposal selected to be carried out under subsection (d).

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: 2 Inclusion
children.text: The cost of carrying out ecological restoration treatments as provided in paragraph (1) may, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, include cancellation and termination costs required to be obligated for contracts to carry out ecological restoration treatments on National Forest System land for each proposal selected to be carried out under subsection (d).

children.heading: Contents
children.label: 3 Contents
children.text: The Fund shall consist of such amounts as are appropriated to the Fund under paragraph (6).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On request by the Secretary, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer from the Fund to the Secretary such amounts as the Secretary determines are appropriate, in accordance with paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: during a period of more than 10 fiscal years; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in excess of $4,000,000 in any 1 fiscal year.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: The limitation described in subparagraph (B)(i) shall not apply to a proposal for which a 1-time extension is granted under subsection (d)(4).

children.heading: Accounting and reporting system
children.label: 5 Accounting and reporting system
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an accounting and reporting system for the Fund.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 6 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Fund $80,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, to remain available until expended.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the manner in which the proposal would be implemented to achieve ecological and community economic benefit, including capacity building to accomplish restoration;

children.label: i
children.text: the anticipated unit treatment cost reductions over 10 years;

children.label: ii
children.text: the anticipated costs for infrastructure needed for the proposal;

children.label: iii
children.text: the projected sustainability of the supply of woody biomass and small-diameter trees removed in ecological restoration treatments; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the projected local economic benefits of the proposal;

children.label: C
children.text: documentation of the non-Federal investment in the priority landscape, including the sources and uses of the investments; and

children.label: D
children.text: a plan to decommission any temporary roads established to carry out the proposal.

children.label: A
children.text: consistent with the proposal and strategy; and

children.label: B
children.text: identified through the collaborative process described in subsection (b)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: a description of all acres (or other appropriate unit) treated and restored through projects implementing the strategy;

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of progress, including performance measures and how prior year evaluations have contributed to improved project performance;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of community benefits achieved, including any local economic benefits;

children.label: D
children.text: the results of the multiparty monitoring, evaluation, and accountability process under paragraph (4); and

children.label: i
children.text: treatments; and

children.label: ii
children.text: relevant fire management activities.

children.heading: Multiparty monitoring
children.label: 4 Multiparty monitoring
children.text: The Secretary shall, in collaboration with the Secretary of the Interior and interested persons, use a multiparty monitoring, evaluation, and accountability process to assess the positive or negative ecological, social, and economic effects of projects implementing a selected proposal for not less than 15 years after project implementation commences.
